_comment: Tight-token base policy (see /css-wisdom tight-token-strategy): forbid Tailwind's default numeric freedom-scale + default color palette; allow semantic tokens (hsp-/vsp-/…), arbitrary values (w-[30px], bg-[hsl(...)]), fractions (w-1/2), keywords (w-full), and 0. NOTE: w-{n}/h-{n}/basis-{n} are intentionally NOT listed — the lint tool strips everything after '/' as an opacity modifier (rules.js), so banning w-{n} would also false-positive the valid fraction w-1/2 (opacity only applies to color utilities, not sizing). Tracked upstream on @takazudo/zudo-design-token-lint; add w-/h-/basis- here once the tool distinguishes fractions from opacity. size-{n}/min-w/max-w/min-h/max-h have no common fraction form and are safe to forbid.
suggestionSuffix: use hsp-*/vsp-* tokens, an arbitrary value like w-[30px], a fraction like w-1/2, or a semantic token
